registry.description: Mechanically determinate lifecycle vocabulary and bindings for GenDev 1.0.

gates.name: Project Initialized
gates.purpose: Confirm the baseline has an active project workspace and identity.

gates.name: Vision Ready
gates.purpose: Establish why the product exists and what success means.

gates.name: Requirements Ready
gates.purpose: Convert the vision into testable product requirements.

gates.name: Architecture Ready
gates.purpose: Define system structure, boundaries, lifecycle, stack, and verification design.

gates.name: Governance Ready
gates.purpose: Make security, policy, identity, audit, and agent/tool behavior explicit.

gates.name: Build Ready
gates.purpose: Plan and execute the bounded phase loop without changing the active major gate.

gates.name: Implementation Ready For Review
gates.purpose: Assemble aggregate implementation and readiness evidence for whole-build review.

gates.name: Acceptance Ready
gates.purpose: Complete final review, remediation, verification, traceability, and implementation acceptance.

gates.name: Deployment Ready
gates.purpose: Complete deployment readiness and operations, then authorize and record deployment or an explicit non-deployment disposition.

gates.name: As-Built Closed
gates.purpose: Preserve the actual project state after deployment or non-deployment disposition.

criteria.description: The project manifest exists and is structurally valid.
The canonical project-wide scaffold exists.
Project name, slug, owner, and initialization date are recorded.
The problem and product purpose are explicit.
Target users are explicit.
Success criteria and read timing are measurable.
Non-goals are explicit.
Assumptions, risks, and blocking questions have dispositions.
Every in-scope requirement has a stable identifier.
Every baseline requirement has acceptance criteria.
C2 and C3 acceptance criteria are mechanically recognizable EARS statements.
C1 may use concrete observable criteria instead of EARS.
Every class states unwanted behavior wherever an error or failure path exists.
Requirements are specific enough to verify and architect.
Architecture rules trace to requirements.
Components, ownership, interfaces, and trust boundaries are explicit.
Runtime, data, state, and failure lifecycles are defined.
The technology-stack decision is Accepted.
A named human approved the verification specification.
Verification cases, commands, and oracles map to G2 criteria.
Failure, scale, and evolution interrogation is proportional to blast radius.
Actors, identities, roles, and permissions are explicit.
Allowed and forbidden authorization behavior is explicit.
Audit events, fields, retention, and ownership are explicit.
Secrets and data sensitivity handling are explicit.
Trust boundaries and external tool access are explicit.
Security and governance negative tests are defined.
Declared phases have stable IDs and explicit order.
Every in-scope requirement maps to an owning phase.
Cross-phase integration criteria and ownership are declared.
The partition rationale bounds each phase to coherent work.
Verification coverage declares metric, target, scope, command, exclusions, owner, and shortfall handling.
The phase build plan defines bounded scope, requirements, and exit conditions.
Test and UAT design is sufficient to define the phase exit proof.
The tactical plan uses stable workstream and task IDs.
Task dependencies and execution order are explicit.
Each task identifies authorized file and module paths.
Each task maps required tests and negative cases.
Each task declares executable verification commands and expected evidence.
Each task declares rollback, recovery, or irreversibility handling.
The construction directive completely binds scope, authority, quality, security, verification, and stop rules.
The build prompt points to the complete directive and tactical task set without becoming separate authority.
The phase test and UAT plan is human-approved.
Every governing upstream artifact is pinned to reviewed bytes.
A named human authorizes bounded implementation.
Implementation evidence binds the exact phase candidate and changed files.
Planned tests and UAT ran with commands, results, and evidence.
Independent review binds the exact phase candidate.
Remediation is Complete or validly not_required by a clean review.
Cumulative traceability records the phase requirement outcomes.
Per-phase as-built closeout is Complete.
Phase learnings are Accepted for downstream planning.
Regression evidence identifies the command, result, and phases covered.
Coverage reports target, actual result, and any accepted shortfall.
Residual findings and risks have valid dispositions.
Applicable amendments are identified and reconciled.
The per-phase value-review path and complete, not_due, or not_applicable disposition are bound.
A named human approves the complete phase exit evidence set.
A valid G5.0 event authorized the declared phase loop.
Every declared, nonsuperseded phase has one valid exit event.
The exact whole-build candidate revision is bound.
Final phase-loop integration and regression criteria passed or have valid residual disposition.
Traceability is current across every declared phase.
Every residual is enumerated and nonwaivable risk has named-human acceptance.
The aggregate implementation summary is Complete.
The exact whole-build candidate revision is recorded.
The changed-file inventory is complete.
Cross-phase integration and regression results are complete.
Deviations and residual findings have dispositions.
The final review package is complete.
Independent final review is Complete at the exact candidate.
Aggregate remediation is Complete or validly not_required.
Final test and UAT evidence is Complete.
Cumulative traceability reflects verified implementation.
Critical findings are closed and major residuals are human-accepted.
Reused Accepted authority is bound byte-identically to its originating event.
Deployment readiness is Accepted through a separate approval event.
The production runbook is Complete.
Release scope and exact candidate are bound.
Environment, configuration, and secrets handling are ready.
Migration and rollback or irreversibility are explicit.
Monitoring, incident response, and operational ownership are explicit.
Value-review triggers, owners, and evidence sources are ready.
A named human deployment approver authorized the production or non-deployment action.
Deployment results or explicit non-deployment disposition are Complete.
Monitoring, rollback, and incident outcomes are recorded.
Project value review has a valid complete, not_due, or not_applicable disposition.
Final traceability reflects actual system state and residuals.
Project-level as-built closeout is Complete and human-approved.

transitions.notes: Enters phase planning. It does not create or complete G5.0.

evidence_categories.content_rule: Only the single canonical Status header changes; every other byte and the line-ending convention are preserved.
Reviewed and resulting Git blob OIDs and portable content digests are equal.
Reviewed and resulting blobs/digests are equal and the originating approval event binds the same path and blob.

invariants.rule: project.current_gate is exactly one declared G0-G9 ID.
phase.phase_position is null before G5, records the most recently completed checkpoint inside G5, and retains the final phase position after the loop closes; it never changes project.current_gate.
phase.current_phase_id is null or names exactly one declared phase.
Phase array order is authoritative and IDs are unique.
approvals.latest_decision summarizes but never replaces a gate-log event.
G9 requires project.status closed, active_role none, and no next gate, role, or artifact.
New 1.0 projects use strict schema version 2; older-version onboarding is explicit and version-bound.

rules.rule: Every typed reference declares exactly one target kind.
Canonical authority and evidence references resolve to registered canonical paths.
Supporting-design references resolve only below docs/project/design/.
Supporting artifacts cannot govern canonical authority.
All typed reference edges participate in one directed acyclic graph; approved depth exceptions do not permit cycles.
Supporting-design references are one level deep by default; greater depth requires a declared, justified, named-human-approved exception.

classes.label: Contained
classes.gate_combination: G1-G4 may combine with explicit content preservation, evidence, named-human approval, and recorded justification.

classes.label: Standard
classes.gate_combination: Full chain by default; a combination requires explicit span, preserved criteria, justification, and named-human approval.

compatibility.legacy_event_policy: Historical unknowns are recorded, never fabricated.

decision_records.checked_statement: I reviewed and accept D-001 through D-018 as encoded in these exact decision-record blobs, including the increased phase-exit evidence, named-human checkpoint and phase-exit controls, NAA authority boundary, and G6-G9 deployment/value/terminal model.
decision_records.risk_disposition: I accept the candidate design risks and acknowledge that constitutional amendment, enforcement implementation, release preparation, publication, and branch pushing remain separately controlled.

decision_records.checked_statement: I reviewed and accept D-001 through D-018 as encoded in these exact decision-record blobs, including the increased phase-exit evidence, named-human checkpoint and phase-exit controls, NAA authority boundary, and G6-G9 deployment/value/terminal model.
decision_records.risk_disposition: I accept the candidate design risks and acknowledge that constitutional amendment, enforcement implementation, release preparation, publication, and branch pushing remain separately controlled.

platform_contract: macOS system Bash 3.2 and BSD utilities
Ubuntu GitHub-hosted runner Bash and GNU utilities
